title: Rechargeable charger for rechargeable devices, and method for charging rechargeable devices
abstract: A charging device (100) for charging portable rechargeable electronic devices (200) is provided. The charging device comprising a rechargeable battery (120) and a charging control circuitry for controlling a charging of at least one portable rechargeable device. The charging control circuitry comprises interface circuitry (110) adapted for electrically connecting the portable rechargeable device (200) to the rechargeable battery (120), so as to charge the portable rechargeable device from power of the rechargeable battery; gauging circuitry (150) adapted for automatically detecting a battery charging level of the connected portable rechargeable device, comparing the detected battery charging level to a threshold value and detecting if the threshold value has been reached; and a processor configured for serving as a control unit (140) connected at least to the interface circuitry (110) and the gauging circuitry (150). The control unit comprises a timer (145). The charging control circuitry is adapted for repetitively performing a charging phase by transferring energy from the rechargeable battery (120) to the portable rechargeable device (200) via the interface circuitry (110) until the gauging circuitry (150) indicates that the threshold value has been reached, entering a pause phase wherein the charging of the portable rechargeable device from the rechargeable battery is suspended, and starting the timer; and when the timer (145) has elapsed, returning to a charging phase.
claims:
Claims Claims A charging device (100) for charging portable rechargeable electronic devices (200), the charging device comprising: a rechargeable battery (120); and charging control circuitry for controlling a charging of at least one portable rechargeable device, the charging control circuitry comprising: interface circuitry (110) adapted for electrically connecting the portable rechargeable device (200) to the rechargeable battery (120), so as to charge the portable rechargeable device from power of the rechargeable battery; gauging circuitry (150) adapted for automatically detecting a battery charging level of the connected portable rechargeable device, comparing the detected battery charging level to a threshold value and detecting if the threshold value has been reached; and a processor configured for serving as a control unit (140) connected at least to the interface circuitry (110) and the gauging circuitry (150), the control unit comprising a timer (145), the charging control circuitry being adapted for repetitively: performing a charging phase by transferring energy from the rechargeable battery (120) to the portable rechargeable device (200) via the interface circuitry (110) until the gauging circuitry (150) indicates that the threshold value has been reached; entering a pause phase wherein the charging of the portable rechargeable device from the rechargeable battery is suspended, and starting the timer; and when the timer (145) has elapsed, returning to a charging phase. The charging device of claim 1 , wherein the timer is configured to elapse after more than one hour. The charging device of claim 1 , wherein the timer is configured to elapse after more than ten hours. The charging device of claim 1 , wherein the gauging circuitry (150) is adapted for measuring a charging current for automatically detecting the battery charging level of the connected portable rechargeable device, and wherein the threshold value is a predefined charging current. 

5. The charging device of claim 1 , further comprising boost circuitry (130), wherein the rechargeable battery (120) and the portable rechargeable device (200) are connected via the boost circuity, and wherein the control unit (140) is adapted for controlling the boost circuitry to electrically disconnect the portable rechargeable device from the rechargeable battery and/or to electrically reconnect the portable rechargeable device to the rechargeable battery. 6. The charging device of claim 5, wherein the electrically disconnecting the portable rechargeable device (200) from the rechargeable battery (120) comprises the control unit (140) controlling the boost circuitry (130) to switch off a charging voltage, wherein the portable rechargeable device (200) comprises second charging circuitry (230) for detecting the switched off charging voltage. 7. The device of claim 1 , wherein the control unit is adapted for detecting that the portable rechargeable device (200) has been connected to or inserted in the charging device, and for thereupon starting a charging phase. 8. A method (301) for charging a portable rechargeable device (200) from a portable charging device (100), the portable charging device (100) having a first rechargeable battery (120) and the portable rechargeable device (200) having a second rechargeable battery (240), the method comprising detecting (310) that the portable rechargeable device has been connected to the portable charging device; upon the detecting, repetitively performing the following steps: o charging (320) the second rechargeable battery (240) of the portable rechargeable device from power of the first rechargeable battery (120); o during charging, automatically detecting (330) a battery charging level of the second rechargeable battery (240) and comparing it to a threshold value; o while the detected battery charging level has not reached the threshold value, continuing the charging (320) of the second rechargeable battery (240); o if the detected battery charging level has reached the threshold value, performing the steps of: â electrically disconnecting (340) the portable rechargeable device from the first rechargeable battery, wherein the charging is disrupted; â starting (350) a timer in the portable charging device; and â when the timer elapses, electrically reconnecting (360) the portable rechargeable device to the first rechargeable battery so as to charge the second rechargeable battery (240). 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the timer elapses after more than one hour, and preferably after more than ten hours. 10. The method of claim 8, wherein the battery charging level of the second rechargeable battery (240) is detected by gauging circuitry measuring a charging current, and wherein the threshold value is a predefined charging current. 11. The method of claim 8, further comprising, during the charging (320), steps of deter- mining a timer value and adjusting the timer according to the timer value. 12. The method of claim 11 , wherein the timer value is determined based on a charging current measured by the gauging circuitry. 13. The method of claim 8, wherein the steps of automatically detecting (330) a battery charging level of the second rechargeable battery (240) of the portable rechargeable device and comparing (340) the detected battery charging level to a threshold value are performed continuously until the threshold is reached. 14. The method of claim 8, wherein the steps of automatically detecting (330) a battery charging level of the second rechargeable battery (240) of the portable rechargeable device and comparing (340) the detected battery charging level to a threshold value are periodically repeated until the threshold is reached. 15. The charging device of claim 1 , further comprising connection sensing means for detecting whether a portable rechargeable electronic device (200) is connected to the charging device (100), regardless of whether the charging device (100) is currently in a pause phase or in a charging phase. 16. The charging device of claim 1 , further comprising connection sensing means enabling a rechargeable electronic device (200) to detect whether it is connected to the charging device (100), regardless of whether the charging device (100) is currently in a pause phase or in a charging phase.
